solution_text: Let {tex}y=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x} \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2}}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex} ... {tex}\left[\because \frac{d}{d x}(\cos x)=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{4-\frac{\left(\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} x+2 \sin x \cos x\right)}{2}}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(\cos x-\sin x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{-1 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{1-\sin 2 x}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(\cos x-\sin x){/tex} ...[ {tex}\because{/tex} 1 - sin2x = (cosx - sinx) 2 = cos 2 x + sin 2 x - 2sinxcos x] {tex}=\frac{-1(\cos x-\sin x)}{(\cos x-\sin x)}{/tex} {tex}=-1{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x} \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{2}}\right)^{2}}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex}     ...{tex}\left[\because \frac{d}{d x}(\cos x)=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{4-\frac{\left(\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} x+2 \sin x \cos x\right)}{2}}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(\cos x-\sin x){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-1 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{1-\sin 2 x}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(\cos x-\sin x){/tex}  ...[{tex}\because{/tex} 1 - sin2x = (cosx - sinx)2 = cos2x + sin2x - 2sinxcos x]
{tex}=\frac{-1(\cos x-\sin x)}{(\cos x-\sin x)}{/tex}
{tex}=-1{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}}),~~where~-\frac{\pi}{4}<x<\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
question_html: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}}),~~where~-\frac{\pi}{4}<x<\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}y=\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}}){/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x} \tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}}){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{1+\sqrt{\left(\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right)^{2}}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right]^{12}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \frac{d}{d x}\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)=\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{1+\{\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}}\}^{2}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right]^{\frac{1}{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{1+\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right]^{-1 / 2} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{1+\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right]^{\frac{-1}{2}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\frac{1+\cos x+1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)} \cdot \frac{(1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)}\right]^{-1 / 2}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\frac{1+\cos x+1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x} \cdot \frac{1-\cos x}{1-\cos x}\right]^{\frac{-1}{2}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{(1+\cos x) \cdot \sin x+(1-\cos x) \cdot \sin x}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{(1+\cos x)}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)^{2}}{\left(1-\cos ^{2} x\right)}\right]^{-1 / 2}\left[\frac{\sin x(1+\cos x+1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{(1+\cos x)}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)^{2}}{\left(1-\cos ^{2} x\right)}\right]^{-1 / 2}\left[\frac{\sin x(1+\cos x+1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{(1+\cos x)}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)^{2}}{\sin x}\right]^{-12} \frac{2 \sin x}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1+\cos x}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)^{2}}{\sin ^{2} x}\right]^{\frac{-1}{2}} \cdot \frac{2 \sin x}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{(1+\cos x)}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\sin x}{(1-\cos x)} \cdot \frac{2 \sin x}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2 \sin ^{2} x}{4(1+\cos x)(1-\cos x)}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\sin ^{2} x}{\left(1-\cos ^{2} x\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\sin ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y=\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}}){/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x} \tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}}){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{1+\sqrt{\left(\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right)^{2}}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right]^{12}{/tex}{tex}\left[\because \frac{d}{d x}\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)=\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{1+\{\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}}\}^{2}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right]^{\frac{1}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{1+\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right]^{-1 / 2} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{1+\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right]^{\frac{-1}{2}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\frac{1+\cos x+1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)} \cdot \frac{(1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)}\right]^{-1 / 2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\frac{1+\cos x+1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x} \cdot \frac{1-\cos x}{1-\cos x}\right]^{\frac{-1}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{(1+\cos x) \cdot \sin x+(1-\cos x) \cdot \sin x}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(1+\cos x)}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)^{2}}{\left(1-\cos ^{2} x\right)}\right]^{-1 / 2}\left[\frac{\sin x(1+\cos x+1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(1+\cos x)}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)^{2}}{\left(1-\cos ^{2} x\right)}\right]^{-1 / 2}\left[\frac{\sin x(1+\cos x+1-\cos x)}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(1+\cos x)}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)^{2}}{\sin x}\right]^{-12} \frac{2 \sin x}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1+\cos x}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{(1-\cos x)^{2}}{\sin ^{2} x}\right]^{\frac{-1}{2}} \cdot \frac{2 \sin x}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(1+\cos x)}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\sin x}{(1-\cos x)} \cdot \frac{2 \sin x}{(1+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2 \sin ^{2} x}{4(1+\cos x)(1-\cos x)}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\sin ^{2} x}{\left(1-\cos ^{2} x\right)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\sin ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}(\sec x+\tan x);~ where~-\frac{\pi}{2}<x<\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
question_html: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}(\sec x+\tan x);~ where~-\frac{\pi}{2}<x<\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
solution_text: Let y = tan -1 (sec x + tan x) {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x} \tan ^{-1}(\sec x+\tan x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{1+(\sec x+\tan x)^{2}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\sec x+\tan x) ~~~~...\left[\because \frac{d}{d x}\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)=\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{1+\sec ^{2} x+\tan ^{2} x+2 \sec x \cdot \tan x} \cdot\left(\sec x \cdot \tan x+\sec ^{2} x\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\left(\sec ^{2} x+\sec ^{2} x+2 \sec x \cdot \tan x\right)} \cdot \sec x \cdot(\sec x+\tan x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2 \sec x(\tan x+\sec x)} \cdot \sec x(\sec x+\tan x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let y = tan-1(sec x + tan x)
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x} \tan ^{-1}(\sec x+\tan x){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{1+(\sec x+\tan x)^{2}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(\sec x+\tan x) ~~~~...\left[\because \frac{d}{d x}\left(\tan ^{-1} x\right)=\frac{1}{1+x^{2}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{1+\sec ^{2} x+\tan ^{2} x+2 \sec x \cdot \tan x} \cdot\left(\sec x \cdot \tan x+\sec ^{2} x\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\left(\sec ^{2} x+\sec ^{2} x+2 \sec x \cdot \tan x\right)} \cdot \sec x \cdot(\sec x+\tan x){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2 \sec x(\tan x+\sec x)} \cdot \sec x(\sec x+\tan x){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a \cos x-b \sin x}{b \cos x+a \sin x}\right);~where ~-\frac{\pi}{2}<x<\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{a}{b} \tan x>-1{/tex}
question_html: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a \cos x-b \sin x}{b \cos x+a \sin x}\right);~where ~-\frac{\pi}{2}<x<\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{a}{b} \tan x>-1{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a \cos x-b \sin x}{b \cos x+a \sin x}\right){/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\frac{a \cos x}{b \cos x}-\frac{b \sin x}{b \cos x}}{\frac{b \cos x}{b \cos x}+\frac{a \sin x}{b \cos x}}\right]=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\frac{a}{b}-\tan x}{1+\frac{a}{b} \tan x}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1} \frac{a}{b}-\tan ^{-1} \tan x{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \tan ^{-1} x-\tan ^{-1} y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-y}{1+x y}\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1} \frac{a}{b}-x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{a}{b}\right)-\frac{d}{d x}(x){/tex} {tex}=0-1 ~~~~~~...\left[\because \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)=0\right]{/tex} = -1
solution_html:
Let {tex}y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a \cos x-b \sin x}{b \cos x+a \sin x}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\frac{a \cos x}{b \cos x}-\frac{b \sin x}{b \cos x}}{\frac{b \cos x}{b \cos x}+\frac{a \sin x}{b \cos x}}\right]=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\frac{a}{b}-\tan x}{1+\frac{a}{b} \tan x}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1} \frac{a}{b}-\tan ^{-1} \tan x{/tex}   {tex}\left[\because \tan ^{-1} x-\tan ^{-1} y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-y}{1+x y}\right)\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1} \frac{a}{b}-x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{a}{b}\right)-\frac{d}{d x}(x){/tex}
{tex}=0-1 ~~~~~~...\left[\because \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)=0\right]{/tex}
= -1

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\sec ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{4 x^{3}-3 x}\right), 0<x<\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
question_html: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\sec ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{4 x^{3}-3 x}\right), 0<x<\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}y=\sec ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{4 x^{3}-3 x}\right){/tex} ..... (i) On putting x = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} in eq. (i), we get {tex}y=\sec ^{-1} \frac{1}{4 \cos ^{3} \theta-3 \cos \theta}{/tex} {tex}=\sec ^{-1} \frac{1}{\cos 3 \theta}{/tex} {tex}=\sec ^{-1}(\sec 3 \theta)=3 \theta{/tex} {tex}=3 \cos ^{-1} x\left[\because \theta=\cos ^{-1} x\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(3 \cos ^{-1} x\right){/tex} {tex}=3 \cdot \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y=\sec ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{4 x^{3}-3 x}\right){/tex} ..... (i)
On putting x = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} in eq. (i), we get
{tex}y=\sec ^{-1} \frac{1}{4 \cos ^{3} \theta-3 \cos \theta}{/tex}
{tex}=\sec ^{-1} \frac{1}{\cos 3 \theta}{/tex}
{tex}=\sec ^{-1}(\sec 3 \theta)=3 \theta{/tex}
{tex}=3 \cos ^{-1} x\left[\because \theta=\cos ^{-1} x\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(3 \cos ^{-1} x\right){/tex}
{tex}=3 \cdot \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 a^{2} x-x^{3}}{a^{3}-3 a x^{2}}\right), \frac{-1}{\sqrt{3}}<\frac{x}{a}<\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
question_html: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 a^{2} x-x^{3}}{a^{3}-3 a x^{2}}\right), \frac{-1}{\sqrt{3}}<\frac{x}{a}<\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 a^{2} x-x^{3}}{a^{3}-3 a x^{2}}\right){/tex} Put {tex}x=a \tan \theta \Rightarrow \theta=\tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}{/tex} {tex}\therefore y=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{3 \tan \theta-\tan ^{3} \theta}{1-3 \tan ^{2} \theta}\right]{/tex} ... {tex}\left[\because \tan 3 \theta=\frac{3 \tan \theta-\tan ^{3} \theta}{1-3 \tan ^{2} \theta}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1}(\tan 3 \theta)=3 \theta{/tex} {tex}=3 \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}~~~~~~~...\left[\because \theta=\tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=3 \cdot \frac{d}{d x} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}=3 \cdot\left[\frac{1}{1+\frac{x^{2}}{a^{2}}}\right] \cdot \frac{d}{d x} \cdot\left(\frac{x}{a}\right){/tex} {tex}=3 \cdot \frac{a^{2}}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a}=\frac{3 a}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=3 \cdot \frac{a^{2}}{a^{2}+x^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{a}=\frac{3 a}{a^{2}+x^{2}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 a^{2} x-x^{3}}{a^{3}-3 a x^{2}}\right){/tex}
Put {tex}x=a \tan \theta \Rightarrow \theta=\tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore y=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{3 \tan \theta-\tan ^{3} \theta}{1-3 \tan ^{2} \theta}\right]{/tex}       ...{tex}\left[\because \tan 3 \theta=\frac{3 \tan \theta-\tan ^{3} \theta}{1-3 \tan ^{2} \theta}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1}(\tan 3 \theta)=3 \theta{/tex}
{tex}=3 \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}~~~~~~~...\left[\because \theta=\tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=3 \cdot \frac{d}{d x} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}=3 \cdot\left[\frac{1}{1+\frac{x^{2}}{a^{2}}}\right] \cdot \frac{d}{d x} \cdot\left(\frac{x}{a}\right){/tex}
{tex}=3 \cdot \frac{a^{2}}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a}=\frac{3 a}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=3 \cdot \frac{a^{2}}{a^{2}+x^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{a}=\frac{3 a}{a^{2}+x^{2}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)-1<x<1, x \neq 0{/tex}
question_html: Differentiate w.r.t. x: {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)-1<x<1, x \neq 0{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}\right){/tex} Put {tex}x^{2}=\cos 2 \theta{/tex} {tex}\therefore y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+\cos 2 \theta}+\sqrt{1-\cos 2 \theta}}{\sqrt{1+\cos 2 \theta}-\sqrt{1-\cos 2 \theta}}\right){/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+2 \cos ^{2} \theta-1}+\sqrt{1-1+2 \sin ^{2} \theta}}{\sqrt{1+2 \cos ^{2} \theta-1}-\sqrt{1-1+2 \sin ^{2} \theta}}\right){/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2} \cos \theta+\sqrt{2} \sin \theta}{\sqrt{2} \cos \theta-\sqrt{2} \sin \theta}\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{2}(\cos \theta+\sin \theta)}{\sqrt{2}(\cos \theta-\sin \theta)}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\cos \theta+\sin \theta}{\cos \theta-\sin \theta}\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\frac{\cos \theta+\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}}{\frac{\cos \theta-\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}}\right){/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1+\tan \theta}{1-\tan \theta}\right){/tex} {tex}=\tan ^{-1} \tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta\right) ~~~...\left[\because \tan (a+b)=\frac{\tan a+\tan b}{1-\tan a \cdot \tan b}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x^{2}{/tex} ... {tex}\left[\because 2 \theta=\cos ^{-1} x^{2} \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x^{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x^{2}\right){/tex} {tex}=0+\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x} x^{2}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{\sqrt{1+x^{2}-\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}}\right){/tex}
Put {tex}x^{2}=\cos 2 \theta{/tex}
{tex}\therefore y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+\cos 2 \theta}+\sqrt{1-\cos 2 \theta}}{\sqrt{1+\cos 2 \theta}-\sqrt{1-\cos 2 \theta}}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+2 \cos ^{2} \theta-1}+\sqrt{1-1+2 \sin ^{2} \theta}}{\sqrt{1+2 \cos ^{2} \theta-1}-\sqrt{1-1+2 \sin ^{2} \theta}}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2} \cos \theta+\sqrt{2} \sin \theta}{\sqrt{2} \cos \theta-\sqrt{2} \sin \theta}\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{2}(\cos \theta+\sin \theta)}{\sqrt{2}(\cos \theta-\sin \theta)}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\cos \theta+\sin \theta}{\cos \theta-\sin \theta}\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\frac{\cos \theta+\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}}{\frac{\cos \theta-\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1+\tan \theta}{1-\tan \theta}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\tan ^{-1} \tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta\right) ~~~...\left[\because \tan (a+b)=\frac{\tan a+\tan b}{1-\tan a \cdot \tan b}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x^{2}{/tex}    ...{tex}\left[\because 2 \theta=\cos ^{-1} x^{2} \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x^{2}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)+\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x^{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}=0+\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}} \cdot \frac{d}{d x} x^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-x}{\sqrt{1-x^{4}}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} of the functions: {tex}x=t+\frac{1}{t}, y=t-\frac{1}{t}{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} of the functions: {tex}x=t+\frac{1}{t}, y=t-\frac{1}{t}{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\because x = t + \frac{1}{t}{/tex} and {tex}y = t - \frac{1}{t}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {t + \frac{1}{t}} \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {t - \frac{1}{t}} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = 1+\left( { - 1} \right){t^{ - 2}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = 1 - \left( { - 1} \right){t^{ - 2}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = 1 - \frac{1}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} and {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = 1 + \frac{1}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{{{t^2} - 1}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} and {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{{t^2} + 1/{t^2}}}{{{t^2} - 1/{t^2}}} = \frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{{t^2} - 1}}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\because x = t + \frac{1}{t}{/tex} and {tex}y = t - \frac{1}{t}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {t + \frac{1}{t}} \right){/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {t - \frac{1}{t}} \right){/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = 1+\left( { - 1} \right){t^{ - 2}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = 1 - \left( { - 1} \right){t^{ - 2}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = 1 - \frac{1}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} and {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = 1 + \frac{1}{{{t^2}}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{{{t^2} - 1}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} and {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{{t^2} + 1/{t^2}}}{{{t^2} - 1/{t^2}}} = \frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{{t^2} - 1}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} functions: x = {tex}e^{\theta}\left(\theta+\frac{1}{\theta}\right){/tex} , y = {tex}e^{-\theta}\left(\theta-\frac{1}{\theta}\right){/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} functions: x = {tex}e^{\theta}\left(\theta+\frac{1}{\theta}\right){/tex}, y = {tex}e^{-\theta}\left(\theta-\frac{1}{\theta}\right){/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}e^{\theta}\left(\theta+\frac{1}{\theta}\right){/tex} and y = {tex}e^{-\theta}\left(\theta-\frac{1}{\theta}\right){/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left[ {{e^{ \theta }}.\left( {\theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= {e^{ \theta }}.\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {\theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right) + \left( {\theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right).\frac{d}{{d\theta }}{e^\theta }{/tex} {tex} = {e^{ \theta }}\left( {1 - \frac{1}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right){e^\theta }{/tex} {tex}= {e^{ \theta }}\left( {1 - \frac{1}{{{\theta ^2}}} + \theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right){/tex} {tex}= {e^{ \theta }}\left( {\frac{{{\theta ^2} - 1 + {\theta ^3} + \theta }}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right){/tex} ...(i) and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left[ {{e^{ - \theta }}.\left( {\theta - \frac{1}{\theta }} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= {e^{ - \theta }}.\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {\theta - \frac{1}{\theta }} \right) + (\theta-\frac{1}{\theta}).\frac{d}{{d\theta }}{e^{ - \theta }}{/tex} {tex}= {e^{ - \theta }}\left( {1 + \frac{1}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\theta - \frac{1}{\theta }} \right){e^{ - \theta }}.\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( { - \theta } \right){/tex} {tex}= {e^{ - \theta }}\left[ {\frac{{{\theta ^2} + 1}}{{{\theta ^2}}} - \frac{{{\theta ^2} - 1}}{\theta }} \right] = {e^{ - \theta }}\left[ {\frac{{{\theta ^2} + 1 - {\theta ^3} + \theta }}{\theta^2 }} \right]{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }}=\frac{{{e^{ - \theta }}\left( {\frac{{{\theta ^2} + 1 - {\theta ^3} + \theta }}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right)}}{{{e^\theta }\left( {\frac{{{\theta ^2} -1 +{\theta ^3} + \theta }}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= {e^{ - 2\theta }}\left( {\frac{{ - {\theta ^3} + {\theta ^2} + \theta + 1}}{{{\theta ^3} + {\theta ^2} + \theta - 1}}} \right){/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\therefore{/tex}x = {tex}e^{\theta}\left(\theta+\frac{1}{\theta}\right){/tex} and y = {tex}e^{-\theta}\left(\theta-\frac{1}{\theta}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left[ {{e^{ \theta }}.\left( {\theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= {e^{ \theta }}.\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {\theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right) + \left( {\theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right).\frac{d}{{d\theta }}{e^\theta }{/tex}
{tex} = {e^{ \theta }}\left( {1 - \frac{1}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right){e^\theta }{/tex}
{tex}= {e^{ \theta }}\left( {1 - \frac{1}{{{\theta ^2}}} + \theta + \frac{1}{\theta }} \right){/tex}
{tex}= {e^{ \theta }}\left( {\frac{{{\theta ^2} - 1 + {\theta ^3} + \theta }}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right){/tex} ...(i)
and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left[ {{e^{ - \theta }}.\left( {\theta - \frac{1}{\theta }} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= {e^{ - \theta }}.\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {\theta - \frac{1}{\theta }} \right) + (\theta-\frac{1}{\theta}).\frac{d}{{d\theta }}{e^{ - \theta }}{/tex}
{tex}= {e^{ - \theta }}\left( {1 + \frac{1}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\theta - \frac{1}{\theta }} \right){e^{ - \theta }}.\frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( { - \theta } \right){/tex}
{tex}= {e^{ - \theta }}\left[ {\frac{{{\theta ^2} + 1}}{{{\theta ^2}}} - \frac{{{\theta ^2} - 1}}{\theta }} \right] = {e^{ - \theta }}\left[ {\frac{{{\theta ^2} + 1 - {\theta ^3} + \theta }}{\theta^2 }} \right]{/tex} ...(ii)
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }}=\frac{{{e^{ - \theta }}\left( {\frac{{{\theta ^2} + 1 - {\theta ^3} + \theta }}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right)}}{{{e^\theta }\left( {\frac{{{\theta ^2} -1 +{\theta ^3} + \theta }}{{{\theta ^2}}}} \right)}}{/tex}
{tex}= {e^{ - 2\theta }}\left( {\frac{{ - {\theta ^3} + {\theta ^2} + \theta + 1}}{{{\theta ^3} + {\theta ^2} + \theta - 1}}} \right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} of the functions: {tex}x=3 \cos \theta-2 \cos ^{3} \theta, y=3 \sin \theta-2 \sin ^{3} \theta{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} of the functions: {tex}x=3 \cos \theta-2 \cos ^{3} \theta, y=3 \sin \theta-2 \sin ^{3} \theta{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\because x = 3\cos \theta - 2{\cos ^3}\theta {/tex} and {tex}y = 3\sin \theta - 2{\sin ^3}\theta {/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta}} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {3\cos \theta } \right) - \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {2{{\cos }^3}\theta } \right){/tex} {tex}= 3.\left( { - \sin \theta } \right) - 2.3{\cos ^2}\theta .\frac{d}{{d\theta }}.\cos \theta {/tex} {tex} = - 3\sin \theta + 6{\cos ^2}\theta \sin \theta {/tex} and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = 3\cos\theta - 2.3\ {\sin ^2}\theta .\frac{d}{{d\theta }}.\sin \theta {/tex} {tex} = 3\cos \theta - 6{\sin ^2}\theta \cos \theta {/tex} Now, {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = \frac{{3\cos \theta - 6{{\sin }^2}\theta \cos \theta }}{{ - 3\sin \theta + 6{{\cos }^2}\theta \sin \theta }}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{3\cos \theta \left( {1 - 2{{\sin }^2}\theta } \right)}}{{3\sin \theta \left( { - 1 + 2{{\cos }^2}\theta } \right)}} = \cot \theta .\frac{{\cos 2\theta }}{{\cos 2\theta }} = \cot \theta {/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\because x = 3\cos \theta - 2{\cos ^3}\theta {/tex} and {tex}y = 3\sin \theta - 2{\sin ^3}\theta {/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{d\theta}} = \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {3\cos \theta } \right) - \frac{d}{{d\theta }}\left( {2{{\cos }^3}\theta } \right){/tex}

{tex}= 3.\left( { - \sin \theta } \right) - 2.3{\cos ^2}\theta .\frac{d}{{d\theta }}.\cos \theta {/tex}

{tex} = - 3\sin \theta + 6{\cos ^2}\theta \sin \theta {/tex}

and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{d\theta }} = 3\cos\theta - 2.3\ {\sin ^2}\theta .\frac{d}{{d\theta }}.\sin \theta {/tex}

{tex} = 3\cos \theta - 6{\sin ^2}\theta \cos \theta {/tex}

Now, {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/d\theta }}{{dx/d\theta }} = \frac{{3\cos \theta - 6{{\sin }^2}\theta \cos \theta }}{{ - 3\sin \theta + 6{{\cos }^2}\theta \sin \theta }}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{3\cos \theta \left( {1 - 2{{\sin }^2}\theta } \right)}}{{3\sin \theta \left( { - 1 + 2{{\cos }^2}\theta } \right)}} = \cot \theta .\frac{{\cos 2\theta }}{{\cos 2\theta }} = \cot \theta {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} from the functions: {tex}\sin x=\frac{2 t}{1+t^{2}}, \ \tan y=\frac{2 t}{1-t^{2}}{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} from the functions: {tex}\sin x=\frac{2 t}{1+t^{2}}, \ \tan y=\frac{2 t}{1-t^{2}}{/tex}
solution_text: Here, {tex} \sin x=\frac{2 t}{1+t^{2}}{/tex} .... (i) And {tex}\tan y=\frac{2 t}{1-t^{2}}{/tex} .... (ii) {tex}\therefore \frac{d}{d x} \sin x \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t}\left(\frac{2 t}{1+t^{2}}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos x \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{\left(1+t^{2}\right) \cdot \frac{d}{d t}(2 t)-(2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t}\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2\left(1+t^{2}\right)-2 t \cdot 2 t}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}=\frac{2+2 t^{2}-4 t^{2}}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{2\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\cos x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{2\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} x}}=\frac{2\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{2 t}{1+t^{2}}\right)^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{2\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)}=\frac{2}{1+t^{2}}{/tex} ..... (iii) Also, {tex}\frac{d}{d y} \tan y \cdot \frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t}\left(\frac{2 t}{1-t^{2}}\right){/tex} {tex}\sec ^{2} y \frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{\left(1-t^{2}\right) \frac{d}{d t} \cdot(2 t)-2 t \cdot \frac{d}{d t}\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{2-2 t^{2}+4 t^{2}}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sec ^{2} y}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\left(1+\tan ^{2} y\right)}=\frac{2\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{1+\frac{4 t^{2}}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}=\frac{2}{1+t^{2}}{/tex} .... (iv) {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d t}{d x / d t}=\frac{2 / 1+t^{2}}{2 / 1+t^{2}}=1{/tex} [from eqs. (iii) and (iv)]
solution_html:
Here, {tex} \sin x=\frac{2 t}{1+t^{2}}{/tex} .... (i)
And {tex}\tan y=\frac{2 t}{1-t^{2}}{/tex} .... (ii)
{tex}\therefore \frac{d}{d x} \sin x \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t}\left(\frac{2 t}{1+t^{2}}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos x \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{\left(1+t^{2}\right) \cdot \frac{d}{d t}(2 t)-(2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t}\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2\left(1+t^{2}\right)-2 t \cdot 2 t}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}=\frac{2+2 t^{2}-4 t^{2}}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{2\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\cos x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{2\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} x}}=\frac{2\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\left(\frac{2 t}{1+t^{2}}\right)^{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{2\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)}=\frac{2}{1+t^{2}}{/tex} ..... (iii)
Also, {tex}\frac{d}{d y} \tan y \cdot \frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t}\left(\frac{2 t}{1-t^{2}}\right){/tex}
{tex}\sec ^{2} y \frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{\left(1-t^{2}\right) \frac{d}{d t} \cdot(2 t)-2 t \cdot \frac{d}{d t}\left(1-t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{2-2 t^{2}+4 t^{2}}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sec ^{2} y}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\left(1+\tan ^{2} y\right)}=\frac{2\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{1+\frac{4 t^{2}}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}} \cdot \frac{\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2}}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)^{2}}=\frac{2}{1+t^{2}}{/tex} .... (iv)
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d t}{d x / d t}=\frac{2 / 1+t^{2}}{2 / 1+t^{2}}=1{/tex} [from eqs. (iii) and (iv)]

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} of the functions: {tex}x = \frac{{1 + \log t}}{{{t^2}}},y = \frac{{3 + 2\log t}}{t}{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} of the functions:  {tex}x = \frac{{1 + \log t}}{{{t^2}}},y = \frac{{3 + 2\log t}}{t}{/tex}
solution_text: Here, {tex} x = \frac{{1 + \log t}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} and {tex}y = \frac{{3 + 2\log t}}{t}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{{{t^2}.\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {1 + \log t} \right) - \left( {1 + \log t} \right).\frac{d}{{dt}}{t^2}}}{{{{\left( {{t^2}} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{t^2}.\frac{1}{t} - \left( {1 + \log t} \right).2t}}{{{t^4}}} = \frac{{t - \left( {1 + \log t} \right).2t}}{{{t^4}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{t}{{{t^4}}}\left[ {1 - 2\left( {1 + \log t} \right)} \right] = \frac{{ - 1 - 2\log t}}{{{t^3}}}{/tex} ...(i) and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{t.\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {3 + 2\log t} \right) - \left( {3 + 2\log t} \right).\frac{d}{{dt}}t}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{t.2.\frac{1}{t}-\left( {3 + 2\log t} \right).1}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{2 - 3 - 2\log t}}{{{t^2}}} = \frac{{ - 1 - 2\log t}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{ - 1 - 2\log t/{t^2}}}{{-1 - 2\log t/{t^3}}} = t{/tex}
solution_html:
Here, {tex} x = \frac{{1 + \log t}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} and {tex}y = \frac{{3 + 2\log t}}{t}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{{{t^2}.\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {1 + \log t} \right) - \left( {1 + \log t} \right).\frac{d}{{dt}}{t^2}}}{{{{\left( {{t^2}} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{t^2}.\frac{1}{t} - \left( {1 + \log t} \right).2t}}{{{t^4}}} = \frac{{t - \left( {1 + \log t} \right).2t}}{{{t^4}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{t}{{{t^4}}}\left[ {1 - 2\left( {1 + \log t} \right)} \right] = \frac{{ - 1 - 2\log t}}{{{t^3}}}{/tex} ...(i)

and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{{t.\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {3 + 2\log t} \right) - \left( {3 + 2\log t} \right).\frac{d}{{dt}}t}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{t.2.\frac{1}{t}-\left( {3 + 2\log t} \right).1}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{2 - 3 - 2\log t}}{{{t^2}}} = \frac{{ - 1 - 2\log t}}{{{t^2}}}{/tex} ...(ii)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{ - 1 - 2\log t/{t^2}}}{{-1 - 2\log t/{t^3}}} = t{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: If {tex}x = {e^{\cos 2t}}{/tex} and {tex}y = {e^{\sin 2t}}{/tex} , then prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \frac{{y\log x}}{{x\log y}}{/tex}
question_html: If {tex}x = {e^{\cos 2t}}{/tex} and {tex}y = {e^{\sin 2t}}{/tex}, then prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - \frac{{y\log x}}{{x\log y}}{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\because x = {e^{\cos 2t}}{/tex} and {tex}y = {e^{\sin 2t}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}{e^{\cos 2t}} = {e^{\cos 2t}}.\frac{d}{{dt}}\cos 2t{/tex} {tex}= {e^{\cos 2t}}.\left( { - \sin 2t} \right).\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {2t} \right){/tex} {tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = - 2{e^{\cos 2t}}.\sin 2t{/tex} ....(i) and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}{e^{\sin 2t}} = {e^{\sin 2t}}.\frac{d}{{dt}}\sin 2t{/tex} {tex} = {e^{\sin 2t}}\cos 2t.\frac{d}{{dt}}2t{/tex} {tex}= 2{e^{\sin 2t}}.\cos 2t{/tex} ..(ii) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{dx} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{2{e^{\sin 2t}}.\cos 2t}}{{ - 2{e^{\cos 2t}}.\sin 2t}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{e^{\sin 2t}}.\cos 2t}}{{{e^{\cos 2t}}.\sin 2t}}{/tex} ...(iii) Now, {tex}{/tex} {tex}x =e^{cos2t}{/tex} Taking log on both sides,we get, {tex}\log x = \cos 2t.\log e = \cos 2t{/tex} ...(iv) Also, {tex}y=e^{sin2t}{/tex} Taking log on both sides, we get {tex}\log y = \sin 2t.\log e = \sin 2t{/tex} ...(v) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - y\log x}}{{x\log y}}{/tex} [Using Eqs. (iv) and (v) in Eq. (iii) and {tex}x = {e^{\cos 2t}}{/tex} , {tex}y = {e^{\sin 2t}}{/tex} ] Hence proved.
solution_html:
{tex}\because x = {e^{\cos 2t}}{/tex} and {tex}y = {e^{\sin 2t}}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}{e^{\cos 2t}} = {e^{\cos 2t}}.\frac{d}{{dt}}\cos 2t{/tex}

{tex}= {e^{\cos 2t}}.\left( { - \sin 2t} \right).\frac{d}{{dt}}\left( {2t} \right){/tex}

{tex}\frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = - 2{e^{\cos 2t}}.\sin 2t{/tex}....(i)

and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = \frac{d}{{dt}}{e^{\sin 2t}} = {e^{\sin 2t}}.\frac{d}{{dt}}\sin 2t{/tex}

{tex} = {e^{\sin 2t}}\cos 2t.\frac{d}{{dt}}2t{/tex}

{tex}= 2{e^{\sin 2t}}.\cos 2t{/tex} ..(ii)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{dx} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{2{e^{\sin 2t}}.\cos 2t}}{{ - 2{e^{\cos 2t}}.\sin 2t}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{e^{\sin 2t}}.\cos 2t}}{{{e^{\cos 2t}}.\sin 2t}}{/tex}...(iii)

Now,{tex}{/tex} {tex}x =e^{cos2t}{/tex}

Taking log on both sides,we get,

      {tex}\log x = \cos 2t.\log e = \cos 2t{/tex} ...(iv)

Also, {tex}y=e^{sin2t}{/tex}

Taking log on both sides, we get

 {tex}\log y = \sin 2t.\log e = \sin 2t{/tex}...(v)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - y\log x}}{{x\log y}}{/tex}

[Using Eqs. (iv) and (v) in Eq. (iii) and {tex}x = {e^{\cos 2t}}{/tex}, {tex}y = {e^{\sin 2t}}{/tex}]

Hence proved.

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: If {tex}x = a. sin(2t). (1 + cos2t){/tex} and {tex}y = b .cos2t .(1–cos2t){/tex} , show that {tex}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)_{at \ t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=\frac{b}{a}{/tex}
question_html: If {tex}x = a. sin(2t). (1 + cos2t){/tex} and {tex}y = b .cos2t .(1–cos2t){/tex}, show that {tex}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)_{at \ t=\frac{\pi}{4}}=\frac{b}{a}{/tex}
solution_text: Here, {tex}​​​​​​​x = a. sin(2t).(1 + cos2t) ~~and ~~y = b. cos (2t).(1 - cos 2t){/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d x}{d t}=a\left[\sin 2 t \cdot \frac{d}{d t}(1+\cos 2 t)+(1+\cos 2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t} \sin 2 t\right]{/tex} {tex}=a\left[\sin 2 t .(-\sin 2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t} 2 t+(1+\cos 2 t) \cdot \cos 2 t \cdot \frac{d}{d t} 2 t\right]{/tex} {tex}= -2a .sin^2(2t) + 2a .cos (2t)(1 + cos 2t){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=-2 \alpha\left[\sin ^{2} 2 t-\cos 2 t(1+\cos 2 t)\right]{/tex} .... (i) and {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=b\left[\cos 2 t \cdot \frac{d}{d t}(1-\cos 2 t)+(1-\cos 2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t} \cos 2 t\right]{/tex} {tex}=b\left[\cos 2 t .(\sin 2 t) \frac{d}{d t} 2 t+(1-\cos 2 t)(-\sin 2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t} 2 t\right]{/tex} {tex}=b[2 \sin 2 t \cdot \cos 2 t+2(1-\cos 2 t)(-\sin 2 t)]{/tex} {tex}=b[2 \sin 2 t \cdot \cos 2 t+2(1-\cos 2 t)(-\sin 2 t)]{/tex} ..... (ii) {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d t}{d x / d t}=\frac{-2 b[-\sin 2 t \cdot \cos 2 t+(1-\cos 2 t) \sin 2 t]}{-2 a\left[\sin ^{2} 2 t-\cos 2 t(1+\cos 2 t)\right]}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{b}{a} \cdot \frac{(0+1)}{(1-0)}~~~~...\left[\because \sin \frac{\pi}{2}=1 \text { and } \cos \frac{\pi}{2}=0\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{b}{a}{/tex} Hence proved.
solution_html:
Here,  
{tex}​​​​​​​x = a. sin(2t).(1 + cos2t) ~~and ~~y = b. cos (2t).(1 - cos 2t){/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{d x}{d t}=a\left[\sin 2 t \cdot \frac{d}{d t}(1+\cos 2 t)+(1+\cos 2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t} \sin 2 t\right]{/tex}
{tex}=a\left[\sin 2 t .(-\sin 2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t} 2 t+(1+\cos 2 t) \cdot \cos 2 t \cdot \frac{d}{d t} 2 t\right]{/tex}
{tex}= -2a .sin^2(2t) + 2a .cos (2t)(1 + cos 2t){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d x}{d t}=-2 \alpha\left[\sin ^{2} 2 t-\cos 2 t(1+\cos 2 t)\right]{/tex} .... (i)
and {tex}\frac{d y}{d t}=b\left[\cos 2 t \cdot \frac{d}{d t}(1-\cos 2 t)+(1-\cos 2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t} \cos 2 t\right]{/tex}
{tex}=b\left[\cos 2 t .(\sin 2 t) \frac{d}{d t} 2 t+(1-\cos 2 t)(-\sin 2 t) \cdot \frac{d}{d t} 2 t\right]{/tex}
{tex}=b[2 \sin 2 t \cdot \cos 2 t+2(1-\cos 2 t)(-\sin 2 t)]{/tex}
{tex}=b[2 \sin 2 t \cdot \cos 2 t+2(1-\cos 2 t)(-\sin 2 t)]{/tex} ..... (ii)
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d y / d t}{d x / d t}=\frac{-2 b[-\sin 2 t \cdot \cos 2 t+(1-\cos 2 t) \sin 2 t]}{-2 a\left[\sin ^{2} 2 t-\cos 2 t(1+\cos 2 t)\right]}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{b}{a} \cdot \frac{(0+1)}{(1-0)}~~~~...\left[\because \sin \frac{\pi}{2}=1 \text { and } \cos \frac{\pi}{2}=0\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{b}{a}{/tex} 
Hence proved.

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: If {tex}x = 3\sin t - \sin 3t,y = 3\cos t - \cos 3t,{/tex} find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} at {tex}t = \frac{\pi }{3}{/tex} .
question_html: If {tex}x = 3\sin t - \sin 3t,y = 3\cos t - \cos 3t,{/tex} find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} at {tex}t = \frac{\pi }{3}{/tex}.
solution_text: Here, {tex}x = 3\sin t - \sin 3t,y = 3\cos t - \cos 3t,{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = 3.\frac{d}{{dt}}\sin t - \frac{d}{{dt}}\sin 3t{/tex} {tex} = 3\cos t - \cos 3t.\frac{d}{{dt}}3t = 3\cos t - 3\cos 3t{/tex} ...(i) and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = 3.\frac{d}{{dt}}\cos t - \frac{d}{{dt}}\cos 3t{/tex} {tex}= -3\sin t + \sin 3t.\frac{d}{{dt}}3t{/tex} {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = 3\sin 3t - 3\sin t{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{3\left( {\sin 3t - \sin t} \right)}}{{3\left( {\cos t - \cos 3t} \right)}}{/tex} Now, {tex}{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)_{t = \pi /3}} = \frac{{\sin \frac{{3\pi }}{3} - \sin \frac{\pi }{3}}}{{\left( {\cos \frac{\pi }{3} - \cos 3\frac{\pi }{3}} \right)}} = \frac{{0 - \sqrt 3 /2}}{{\frac{1}{2} - \left( { - 1} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \sqrt 3 /2}}{{3/2}} = \frac{{ - \sqrt 3 }}{3} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sqrt 3 }}{/tex}
solution_html:
Here, {tex}x = 3\sin t - \sin 3t,y = 3\cos t - \cos 3t,{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = 3.\frac{d}{{dt}}\sin t - \frac{d}{{dt}}\sin 3t{/tex}

{tex} = 3\cos t - \cos 3t.\frac{d}{{dt}}3t = 3\cos t - 3\cos 3t{/tex}...(i)

and {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = 3.\frac{d}{{dt}}\cos t - \frac{d}{{dt}}\cos 3t{/tex}

{tex}= -3\sin t + \sin 3t.\frac{d}{{dt}}3t{/tex}

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dt}} = 3\sin 3t - 3\sin t{/tex} ...(ii)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{dy/dt}}{{dx/dt}} = \frac{{3\left( {\sin 3t - \sin t} \right)}}{{3\left( {\cos t - \cos 3t} \right)}}{/tex}

Now, {tex}{\left( {\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right)_{t = \pi /3}} = \frac{{\sin \frac{{3\pi }}{3} - \sin \frac{\pi }{3}}}{{\left( {\cos \frac{\pi }{3} - \cos 3\frac{\pi }{3}} \right)}} = \frac{{0 - \sqrt 3 /2}}{{\frac{1}{2} - \left( { - 1} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - \sqrt 3 /2}}{{3/2}} = \frac{{ - \sqrt 3 }}{3} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sqrt 3 }}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Differentiate {tex}\frac{x}{{\sin x}}{/tex} w.r.t. sin x.
question_html: Differentiate {tex}\frac{x}{{\sin x}}{/tex} w.r.t. sin x.
solution_text: Let {tex}u = \frac{x}{{\sin x}}{/tex} and {tex}v = \sin x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{du}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\sin x.\frac{d}{{dx}}x - x.\frac{d}{{dx}}\sin x}}{{{{\left( {\sin x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{\sin x - x\cos x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x}}{/tex} ...(i) and {tex}\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\sin x = \cos x{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}\therefore \frac{{du}}{{dv}} = \frac{{du/dx}}{{dv/dx}} = \frac{{\sin x - x\cos x/{{\sin }^2}x}}{{\cos x}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\sin x - x\cos x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x\cos x}} = \frac{{\frac{{\sin x - x\cos x}}{{\cos x}}}}{{\frac{{{{\sin }^2}x\cos x}}{{\cos x}}}}{/tex} [Dividing by cos x in both numerator and denominator] {tex} = \frac{{\tan x - x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}u = \frac{x}{{\sin x}}{/tex} and {tex}v = \sin x{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{du}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\sin x.\frac{d}{{dx}}x - x.\frac{d}{{dx}}\sin x}}{{{{\left( {\sin x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{\sin x - x\cos x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x}}{/tex} ...(i)

and {tex}\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\sin x = \cos x{/tex} ...(ii)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{du}}{{dv}} = \frac{{du/dx}}{{dv/dx}} = \frac{{\sin x - x\cos x/{{\sin }^2}x}}{{\cos x}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\sin x - x\cos x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x\cos x}} = \frac{{\frac{{\sin x - x\cos x}}{{\cos x}}}}{{\frac{{{{\sin }^2}x\cos x}}{{\cos x}}}}{/tex}

[Dividing by cos x in both numerator and denominator]

{tex} = \frac{{\tan x - x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Differentiate {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - 1}}{x}} \right){/tex} w.r.t. tan -1 x when {tex}x \ne 0{/tex}
question_html: Differentiate {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - 1}}{x}} \right){/tex}w.r.t. tan-1 x when {tex}x \ne 0{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}u = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - 1}}{x}} \right){/tex} and {tex}v = {\tan ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} {tex}put\ x = \tan \theta {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow u = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta } - 1}}{{\tan \theta }}{/tex} {tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\left( {\sec \theta - 1} \right)\cos \theta }}{{\sin \theta }}{/tex} {tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 - \cos\theta }}{{\sin \theta }}} \right){/tex} {tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{1 - 1 + 2{{\sin }^2}\theta /2}}{{2\sin \theta /2.\cos \theta + 2}}} \right]\,\,\left[ {\because \cos \theta = 1 - 2{{\sin }^2}\theta } \right]{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\tan \frac{\theta }{2}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{\theta }{2} = \frac{1}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{du}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{{dx}}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{1}{2}.\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex} ...(i) and {tex}\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}\therefore \frac{{du}}{{dv}} = \frac{{du/dx}}{{dv/dx}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{1/2\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}}{{1/\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}} = \frac{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}}{{2\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}} = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}u = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {x^2}} - 1}}{x}} \right){/tex} and {tex}v = {\tan ^{ - 1}}x{/tex}

{tex}put\ x = \tan \theta {/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow u = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\sqrt {1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta } - 1}}{{\tan \theta }}{/tex}

{tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\frac{{\left( {\sec \theta - 1} \right)\cos \theta }}{{\sin \theta }}{/tex}

{tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1 - \cos\theta }}{{\sin \theta }}} \right){/tex}

{tex}= {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{1 - 1 + 2{{\sin }^2}\theta /2}}{{2\sin \theta /2.\cos \theta + 2}}} \right]\,\,\left[ {\because \cos \theta = 1 - 2{{\sin }^2}\theta } \right]{/tex}

{tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\tan \frac{\theta }{2}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{\theta }{2} = \frac{1}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{du}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{2}\frac{d}{{dx}}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{1}{2}.\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex}...(i)

and {tex}\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x = \frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex} ...(ii)

{tex}\therefore \frac{{du}}{{dv}} = \frac{{du/dx}}{{dv/dx}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{1/2\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}}{{1/\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}} = \frac{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}}{{2\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}} = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} when x and y are connected by the relation given {tex}\sin \left( {xy} \right) + \frac{x}{y} = {x^2} - y{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} when x and y are connected by the relation given {tex}\sin \left( {xy} \right) + \frac{x}{y} = {x^2} - y{/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}\sin \left( {xy} \right) + \frac{x}{y} = {x^2} - y{/tex} On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\sin xy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{x}{y}} \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}{x^2} - \frac{d}{{dx}}y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos xy.\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {xy} \right) + \frac{{y\frac{d}{{dx}}x - x.\frac{d}{{dx}}y}}{{{y^2}}} = 2x - \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos xy\left[ {x.\frac{d}{{dx}}y + y\frac{d}{{dx}}.x} \right] + \frac{{y - x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}}}{{{y^2}}} = 2x - \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x\cos xy\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y\cos xy + \frac{y}{{{y^2}}} - \frac{x}{{{y^2}}}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 2x - \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {x\cos xy - \frac{x}{{{y^2}}} + 1} \right] = 2x - y\cos xy - \frac{1}{{{y}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {\frac{{xy^2cosxy-x+y^2 }}{y^2}} \right]=\left[ {\frac{{{2xy-y^2cosxy-1}}}{{{y}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left( {2xy - {y^2}\cos xy - 1} \right)y}}{{\left( {x{y^2}\cos xy - x + {y^2}} \right)}}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}\sin \left( {xy} \right) + \frac{x}{y} = {x^2} - y{/tex}

On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get

{tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\sin xy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{x}{y}} \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}{x^2} - \frac{d}{{dx}}y{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \cos xy.\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {xy} \right) + \frac{{y\frac{d}{{dx}}x - x.\frac{d}{{dx}}y}}{{{y^2}}} = 2x - \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \cos xy\left[ {x.\frac{d}{{dx}}y + y\frac{d}{{dx}}.x} \right] + \frac{{y - x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}}}{{{y^2}}} = 2x - \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow x\cos xy\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y\cos xy + \frac{y}{{{y^2}}} - \frac{x}{{{y^2}}}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 2x - \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {x\cos xy - \frac{x}{{{y^2}}} + 1} \right] = 2x - y\cos xy - \frac{1}{{{y}}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {\frac{{xy^2cosxy-x+y^2 }}{y^2}} \right]=\left[ {\frac{{{2xy-y^2cosxy-1}}}{{{y}}}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\left( {2xy - {y^2}\cos xy - 1} \right)y}}{{\left( {x{y^2}\cos xy - x + {y^2}} \right)}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} when x and y are connected by the relation given {tex}\sec \left( {x + y} \right) = xy{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} when x and y are connected by the relation given {tex}\sec \left( {x + y} \right) = xy{/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}\sec \left( {x + y} \right) = xy{/tex} On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\sec \left( {x + y} \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {xy} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right).\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {x + y} \right) = x.\frac{d}{{dx}}y + y.\frac{d}{{dx}}x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right).\left( {1 + \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right) = x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right) + \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right).\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {\sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right) - x} \right] = y - \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right){/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{y - \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right)}}{{\sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right) - x}}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}\sec \left( {x + y} \right) = xy{/tex}

On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get

{tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\sec \left( {x + y} \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {xy} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right).\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {x + y} \right) = x.\frac{d}{{dx}}y + y.\frac{d}{{dx}}x{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right).\left( {1 + \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right) = x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right) + \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right).\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {\sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right) - x} \right] = y - \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right){/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{y - \sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right)}}{{\sec \left( {x + y} \right).\tan \left( {x + y} \right) - x}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} when x and y are connected by the relation given {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = a{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} when x and y are connected by the relation given {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = a{/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = a{/tex} On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( a \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{1 + {{\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)}^2}}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2x + \frac{d}{{dy}}{y^2}.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex} (using chain rule) {tex} \Rightarrow 2y.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - 2x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 2x}}{{2y}} = \frac{{ - x}}{y}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = a{/tex}

On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get

{tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( a \right){/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{1 + {{\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)}^2}}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = 0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 2x + \frac{d}{{dy}}{y^2}.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex}  (using chain rule)

{tex} \Rightarrow 2y.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = - 2x{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 2x}}{{2y}} = \frac{{ - x}}{y}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} when x and y are connected by the relation given {tex}{\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)^2} = xy{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} when x and y are connected by the relation given {tex}{\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)^2} = xy{/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}{\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)^2} = xy{/tex} On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)^2} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {xy} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right).\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = x.\frac{d}{{dx}}y + y.\frac{d}{{dx}}x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)\left( {2x + 2y\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right) = x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2{x^2}.2x + 2{x^2}.2y\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 2{y^2}.2x + 4{y^3}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {4{x^2}y + 4{y^3} - x} \right] = y - 4{x^3} - 4x{y^2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\left( {y - 4{x^3} - 4x{y^2}} \right)}}{{\left( {4{x^2}y + 4{y^3} - x} \right)}}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}{\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)^2} = xy{/tex}

On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get

{tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)^2} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {xy} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right).\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right) = x.\frac{d}{{dx}}y + y.\frac{d}{{dx}}x{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( {{x^2} + {y^2}} \right)\left( {2x + 2y\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}} \right) = x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 2{x^2}.2x + 2{x^2}.2y\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 2{y^2}.2x + 4{y^3}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {4{x^2}y + 4{y^3} - x} \right] = y - 4{x^3} - 4x{y^2}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\left( {y - 4{x^3} - 4x{y^2}} \right)}}{{\left( {4{x^2}y + 4{y^3} - x} \right)}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: If {tex}a{x^2} + 2hxy + b{y^2} + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0{/tex} , then show that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = 1{/tex} .
question_html: If {tex}a{x^2} + 2hxy + b{y^2} + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0{/tex} , then show that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} = 1{/tex} .
solution_text: We have, {tex}a{x^2} + 2hxy + b{y^2} + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0{/tex} ...(i) On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {a{x^2}} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {2hxy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {b{y^2}} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {2gx} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {2fy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( c \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2ax + 2h\left( {x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y.1} \right) + b.2y\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 2g + 2f\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 0 = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {2hx + 2by + 2f} \right] = - 2ax - 2hy - 2g{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 2\left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{{2\left( {hx + by + f} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{{\left( {hx + by + f} \right)}}{/tex} ...(ii) Now, differentiating Eq. (i) w.r.t. y, we get {tex}\frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {a{x^2}} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {2hxy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {b{y^2}} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {2gx} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {2fy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( c \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow a.2x.\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} + 2h.\left( {x.\frac{d}{{dy}}y + y.\frac{d}{{dy}}x} \right) + b.2y + 2g.\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} + 2f + 0 = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}}\left[ {2ax + 2hy + 2g} \right] = - 2hx - 2by - 2f{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}}=\frac{{ - 2\left( {hx + by + f} \right)}}{{2\left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}} = -\frac{{\left( { hx + by + f} \right)}}{{\left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{/tex} ...(iii) LHS= {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}.\frac{{dx}}{{dy}}=\frac{{ - \left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{{\left( {hx + by + f} \right)}}.\frac{{-\left( { hx + by + f} \right)}}{{\left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{/tex} [Using Eqs. (ii) and (iii)] = 1 = RHS Hence Proved.
solution_html:
We have, {tex}a{x^2} + 2hxy + b{y^2} + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0{/tex}...(i)

On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get

{tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {a{x^2}} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {2hxy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {b{y^2}} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {2gx} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {2fy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( c \right) = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 2ax + 2h\left( {x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + y.1} \right) + b.2y\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 2g + 2f\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} + 0 = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {2hx + 2by + 2f} \right] = - 2ax - 2hy - 2g{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - 2\left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{{2\left( {hx + by + f} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - \left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{{\left( {hx + by + f} \right)}}{/tex} ...(ii)

Now, differentiating Eq. (i) w.r.t. y, we get

{tex}\frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {a{x^2}} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {2hxy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {b{y^2}} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {2gx} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( {2fy} \right) + \frac{d}{{dy}}\left( c \right) = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow a.2x.\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} + 2h.\left( {x.\frac{d}{{dy}}y + y.\frac{d}{{dy}}x} \right) + b.2y + 2g.\frac{{dx}}{{dy}} + 2f + 0 = 0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}}\left[ {2ax + 2hy + 2g} \right] = - 2hx - 2by - 2f{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dy}}=\frac{{ - 2\left( {hx + by + f} \right)}}{{2\left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}} = -\frac{{\left( { hx + by + f} \right)}}{{\left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{/tex}...(iii)

LHS={tex}\frac{dy}{dx}.\frac{{dx}}{{dy}}=\frac{{ - \left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{{\left( {hx + by + f} \right)}}.\frac{{-\left( { hx + by + f} \right)}}{{\left( {ax + hy + g} \right)}}{/tex}[Using Eqs. (ii) and (iii)]

= 1 = RHS
Hence Proved.

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: If {tex}x = {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}{/tex} , prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{x - y}}{{x\log x}}{/tex}
question_html: If {tex}x = {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}{/tex}, prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{x - y}}{{x\log x}}{/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}x = {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{d}{{dx}}x = \frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{x/y}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 1 = {e^{x/y}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}(\frac{x}{y}){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 1 = {e^{x/y}}.\left[ {\frac{{y.1 - x.dy/dx}}{{{y^2}}}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {y^2} = y.{e^{x/y}} - x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{e^{x/y}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{e^{x/y}} = y{e^{x/y}} - {y^2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{y\left( {{e^{x/y}} - y} \right)}}{{x.{e^{x/y}}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left( {{e^{x/y}} - y} \right)}}{{{e^{x/y}}.\frac{x}{y}}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{x - y}}{{x.\log x}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\therefore x=e^{\frac{x}{y}}\Rightarrow logx=\frac{x}{y}\right]{/tex} .￼￼ Hence Proved.
solution_html:
We have, {tex}x = {e^{\frac{x}{y}}}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{d}{{dx}}x = \frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{x/y}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 1 = {e^{x/y}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}(\frac{x}{y}){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 1 = {e^{x/y}}.\left[ {\frac{{y.1 - x.dy/dx}}{{{y^2}}}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {y^2} = y.{e^{x/y}} - x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{e^{x/y}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}.{e^{x/y}} = y{e^{x/y}} - {y^2}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{y\left( {{e^{x/y}} - y} \right)}}{{x.{e^{x/y}}}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\left( {{e^{x/y}} - y} \right)}}{{{e^{x/y}}.\frac{x}{y}}}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{x - y}}{{x.\log x}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\therefore x=e^{\frac{x}{y}}\Rightarrow logx=\frac{x}{y}\right]{/tex}.￼￼

Hence Proved.

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: If x y = e x-y , prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{(1+\log y)^{2}}{\log y}{/tex}
question_html: If xy = ex-y, prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{(1+\log y)^{2}}{\log y}{/tex}
solution_text: We have, y x = e y-x , {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log y x = log -x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log y x = log e y-x {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} x log y = y - x {tex}\cdot{/tex} log e = (y - x) [ {tex}\because{/tex} log e = 1] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log y = {tex}\frac{(y-x)}{x}{/tex} ...(i) Now, differentiating w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{d}{d y}{/tex} log y {tex}\cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x} \frac{(y-x)}{x}​​​​​​​{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x \frac{d}{d x}(y-x)-(y-x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x} x}{x^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x\left(\frac{d y}{d x}-1\right)-(y-x)}{x^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2}}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} - x - y + x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\left(\frac{x^{2}}{y}-x\right){/tex} = -y {tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-y^{2}}{x^{2}-x y}=\frac{-y^{2}}{x(x-y)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{y^{2}}{x(y-x)}-\frac{x}{x}=\frac{y^{2}}{x^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\frac{(y-x)}{x}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(1+\log y)^{2}}{\log y}{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} log y = {tex}\frac{y-x}{x}{/tex} log y = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} - 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + log y = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} ] Hence proved.
solution_html:
We have, yx = ey-x,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log yx = log-x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log yx = log ey-x
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} x log y = y - x {tex}\cdot{/tex} loge = (y - x) [{tex}\because{/tex} loge = 1]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log y = {tex}\frac{(y-x)}{x}{/tex} ...(i)
Now, differentiating w.r.t. x, we get
{tex}\frac{d}{d y}{/tex} log y {tex}\cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d}{d x} \frac{(y-x)}{x}​​​​​​​{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x \frac{d}{d x}(y-x)-(y-x) \cdot \frac{d}{d x} x}{x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x\left(\frac{d y}{d x}-1\right)-(y-x)}{x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{x^{2}}{y} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}=x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} - x - y + x
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}\left(\frac{x^{2}}{y}-x\right){/tex} = -y
{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-y^{2}}{x^{2}-x y}=\frac{-y^{2}}{x(x-y)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{y^{2}}{x(y-x)}-\frac{x}{x}=\frac{y^{2}}{x^{2}} \cdot \frac{1}{\frac{(y-x)}{x}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(1+\log y)^{2}}{\log y}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} log y = {tex}\frac{y-x}{x}{/tex} log y = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} - 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + log y = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}]
Hence proved.

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: If {tex}y = {\left( {\cos x} \right)^{{{\left( {\cos x} \right)}^{\left( {\cos x} \right)...\infty }}}}{/tex} , show that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{y^2}\tan x}}{{y\log \cos x - 1}}{/tex}
question_html: If {tex}y = {\left( {\cos x} \right)^{{{\left( {\cos x} \right)}^{\left( {\cos x} \right)...\infty }}}}{/tex} , show that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{{y^2}\tan x}}{{y\log \cos x - 1}}{/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}y = {\left( {\cos x} \right)^{{{\left( {\cos x} \right)}^{\left( {\cos x} \right)...\infty }}}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y = {\left( {\cos x} \right)^y}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \log y = \log {\left( {\cos x} \right)^y}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \log y = y\log \cos x{/tex} On differentiating w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{1}{y}.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = y.\frac{d}{{dx}}\log \cos x + \log \cos x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{y}.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{y}{{\cos x}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}\cos x + \log \cos x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{y}.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{y}{{\cos x}}.(-sinx) + \log \cos x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {\frac{1}{y} - \log \cos x} \right] = \frac{{ - y\sin x}}{{\cos x}} = - y\tan x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - {y^2}\tan x}}{{\left( {1 - y\log \cos x} \right)}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{{y^2}\tan x}}{{y\log \cos x - 1}}{/tex} Hence Proved.
solution_html:
We have, {tex}y = {\left( {\cos x} \right)^{{{\left( {\cos x} \right)}^{\left( {\cos x} \right)...\infty }}}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow y = {\left( {\cos x} \right)^y}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \log y = \log {\left( {\cos x} \right)^y}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \log y = y\log \cos x{/tex}

On differentiating w.r.t. x, we get

{tex}\frac{1}{y}.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = y.\frac{d}{{dx}}\log \cos x + \log \cos x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{y}.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{y}{{\cos x}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}\cos x + \log \cos x.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{y}.\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{y}{{\cos x}}.(-sinx) + \log \cos x\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}}\left[ {\frac{1}{y} - \log \cos x} \right] = \frac{{ - y\sin x}}{{\cos x}} = - y\tan x{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{ - {y^2}\tan x}}{{\left( {1 - y\log \cos x} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{{y^2}\tan x}}{{y\log \cos x - 1}}{/tex} Hence Proved.

subject: Mathematics
book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: If x sin(a + y) + sin a cos(a + y) = 0, then prove that {tex}\frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) } { \sin a }{/tex} .
question_html: If x sin(a + y) + sin a cos(a + y) = 0, then prove that {tex}\frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) } { \sin a }{/tex}.
solution_text: Given, x sin(a + y) + sin a cos(a + y) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x = \frac { - \sin a \cos ( a + y ) } { \sin ( a + y ) }{/tex} On differentiating both sides w.r.t y,we get {tex}\frac { d x } { d y }{/tex} = {tex} - \frac{{\left[ {\sin (a + y)\frac{d}{{dy}}\{ \sin a\cos (a + y)\} - \sin a\cos (a + y)\frac{d}{{dy}}\{ \sin (a + y)\} } \right]}}{{{{\sin }^2}(a + y)}}{/tex} ...[ By using quotient rule of derivative] {tex}= \left\{ \frac { \sin ( a + y ) \cdot \sin a \sin ( a + y )+sina\;cos(a+y)\;cos(a+y) } { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) } \right\}{/tex} {tex}= \frac { \sin a } { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) }{/tex} {sin 2 (a + y) + cos 2 (a + y)} {tex} = \frac { \sin a } { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) } \cdot 1{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 1 \right]{/tex} {tex} \therefore \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) } { \sin a }{/tex} Hence Proved.
solution_html:
Given,  x sin(a + y) + sin a cos(a + y) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x = \frac { - \sin a \cos ( a + y ) } { \sin ( a + y ) }{/tex}
On differentiating both sides w.r.t y,we get
{tex}\frac { d x } { d y }{/tex}= {tex} - \frac{{\left[ {\sin (a + y)\frac{d}{{dy}}\{ \sin a\cos (a + y)\} - \sin a\cos (a + y)\frac{d}{{dy}}\{ \sin (a + y)\} } \right]}}{{{{\sin }^2}(a + y)}}{/tex}   ...[ By using quotient rule of derivative]
{tex}= \left\{ \frac { \sin ( a + y ) \cdot \sin a \sin ( a + y )+sina\;cos(a+y)\;cos(a+y) } { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) } \right\}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { \sin a } { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) }{/tex}{sin2(a + y) + cos2(a + y)}
{tex} = \frac { \sin a } { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) } \cdot 1{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 1 \right]{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } ( a + y ) } { \sin a }{/tex}
Hence Proved.

book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: If {tex}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {y^2}} = a\left( {x - y} \right){/tex} , prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \sqrt {\frac{{1 - {y^2}}}{{1 - {x^2}}}} {/tex}
question_html: If {tex}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {y^2}} = a\left( {x - y} \right){/tex}, prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \sqrt {\frac{{1 - {y^2}}}{{1 - {x^2}}}} {/tex}
solution_text: We have, {tex}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {y^2}} = a\left( {x - y} \right){/tex} On putting {tex}x = \sin \alpha {/tex} and {tex}y = \sin \beta {/tex} we get {tex}\sqrt {1 - {{\sin }^2}\alpha } + \sqrt {1 - {{\sin }^2}\beta } = a\left( {\sin \alpha - \sin \beta } \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \alpha + \cos \beta = a\left( {\sin \alpha - \sin \beta } \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2\cos \frac{{\alpha + \beta }}{2}.\cos \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2} = a\left( {2\cos \frac{{\alpha + \beta }}{2}.\sin \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2}} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2} = a\sin \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cot \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2} = a{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2} = {\cot ^{ - 1}}a{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \alpha - \beta = 2{\cot ^{ - 1}}a{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}x - {\sin ^{ - 1}}y = 2{\cot ^{ - 1}}a\,\left[ {\because x = \sin \alpha \,and\,y = \sin \beta } \right]{/tex} On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get {tex}\frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} - \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {y^2}} }}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\sqrt {1 - {y^2}} }}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} {/tex} . Hence proved.
solution_html:
We have,
{tex}\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} + \sqrt {1 - {y^2}} = a\left( {x - y} \right){/tex}
On putting {tex}x = \sin \alpha {/tex} and {tex}y = \sin \beta {/tex} we get
{tex}\sqrt {1 - {{\sin }^2}\alpha } + \sqrt {1 - {{\sin }^2}\beta } = a\left( {\sin \alpha - \sin \beta } \right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \alpha + \cos \beta = a\left( {\sin \alpha - \sin \beta } \right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2\cos \frac{{\alpha + \beta }}{2}.\cos \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2} = a\left( {2\cos \frac{{\alpha + \beta }}{2}.\sin \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2}} \right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2} = a\sin \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \cot \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2} = a{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{\alpha - \beta }}{2} = {\cot ^{ - 1}}a{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \alpha - \beta = 2{\cot ^{ - 1}}a{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {\sin ^{ - 1}}x - {\sin ^{ - 1}}y = 2{\cot ^{ - 1}}a\,\left[ {\because x = \sin \alpha \,and\,y = \sin \beta } \right]{/tex}
On differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get
{tex}\frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} - \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {y^2}} }}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\sqrt {1 - {y^2}} }}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} {/tex}.
Hence proved.

question_text: If {tex}y = {\tan ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} find {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex} in terms of y alone.
question_html: If {tex}y = {\tan ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} find {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}}{/tex} in terms of y alone.
solution_text: We have, {tex}y = {\tan ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} on differentiating w.r.t.x,we get {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex} again differentiating w.r.t.x,we get, {tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)^{ - 1}}{/tex} {tex}= - 1{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)^{ - 2}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right){/tex} {tex} = - \frac{1}{{{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}^2}}}.2x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 2\tan y}}{{{{\left( {1 + {{\tan }^2}y} \right)}^2}}}\,~~~...\left[ {\because y = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x \Rightarrow \tan y = x} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 2\tan y}}{{{{\left( {{{\sec }^2}y} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} {tex}= - 2tany.cos^4y{/tex} {tex} = - 2\frac{{\sin y}}{{\cos y}}.{\cos ^2}y.{\cos ^2}y{/tex} {tex}= - \sin 2y.{\cos ^2}y\, ~~~...\left[ {\because \sin 2x = 2\sin x\cos x} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}y = {\tan ^{ - 1}}x{/tex} 

on differentiating w.r.t.x,we get

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}{/tex}

again differentiating w.r.t.x,we get,

{tex}\frac{{{d^2}y}}{{d{x^2}}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)^{ - 1}}{/tex}

{tex}= - 1{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)^{ - 2}}.\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right){/tex}

{tex} = - \frac{1}{{{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}^2}}}.2x{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - 2\tan y}}{{{{\left( {1 + {{\tan }^2}y} \right)}^2}}}\,~~~...\left[ {\because y = {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x \Rightarrow \tan y = x} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - 2\tan y}}{{{{\left( {{{\sec }^2}y} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

{tex}= - 2tany.cos^4y{/tex}

{tex} = - 2\frac{{\sin y}}{{\cos y}}.{\cos ^2}y.{\cos ^2}y{/tex}

{tex}= - \sin 2y.{\cos ^2}y\, ~~~...\left[ {\because \sin 2x = 2\sin x\cos x} \right]{/tex}

book: Class 12 - Mathematics - Exemplar Book by NCERT
question_text: Verify Rolle’s theorem for the function: f (x) = x (x – 1) 2 in [0, 1]
question_html: Verify Rolle’s theorem for the function: f (x) = x (x – 1)2 in [0, 1]
solution_text: We have, f(x) = x(x - 1) 2 in [0, 1] Since, f(x) = x(x - 1) 2 is a polynomial function. So, it is continuous in [0, 1]. Now, {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=x \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(x-1)^{2}+(x-1)^{2} \frac{d}{d x} x{/tex} {tex}=x \cdot 2(x-1) \cdot 1+(x-1)^{2}{/tex} = 2x 2 - 2x + x 2 + 1 - 2x = 3x 2 - 4x + 1 which exists in (0, 1) So, f(x) is differentiable in (0, 1) Now f(0) = 0 and f(1) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(0) = f(1) f satisfies the above conditions of Rolle's theorem. Hence, by Rolle's theorem {tex}\exists c \in (0,1){/tex} such that f'(c) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3c 2 - 4c + 1 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3c 2 - 3c - c + 1 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3c(c - 1) -1(c - 1) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (3c - 1)(c - 1) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow c=\frac{1}{3}, 1 \Rightarrow \frac{1}{3} \in(0.1){/tex} Thus, we see that there exists a real number c in the open interval (0, 1). Hence, Rolle's theorem has been verified.
solution_html:
We have, f(x) = x(x - 1)2 in [0, 1]

  1. Since, f(x) = x(x - 1)2 is a polynomial function.
    So, it is continuous in [0, 1].
  2. Now, {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=x \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(x-1)^{2}+(x-1)^{2} \frac{d}{d x} x{/tex}
    {tex}=x \cdot 2(x-1) \cdot 1+(x-1)^{2}{/tex}
    = 2x2 - 2x + x2 + 1 - 2x
    = 3x2 - 4x + 1 which exists in (0, 1)
    So, f(x) is differentiable in (0, 1)
  3. Now f(0) = 0 and f(1) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(0) = f(1)
    f satisfies the above conditions of Rolle's theorem.
    Hence, by Rolle's theorem {tex}\exists c \in (0,1){/tex} such that
    f'(c) = 0
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3c2 - 4c + 1 = 0
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3c2 - 3c - c + 1 = 0
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3c(c - 1) -1(c - 1) = 0
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (3c - 1)(c - 1) = 0
    {tex}\Rightarrow c=\frac{1}{3}, 1 \Rightarrow \frac{1}{3} \in(0.1){/tex}

Thus, we see that there exists a real number c in the open interval (0, 1).
Hence, Rolle's theorem has been verified.

question_text: Verify Rolle’s theorem for the functions: {tex}f(x)=\sin ^{4} x+\cos ^{4} x \text { in }\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex}
question_html: Verify Rolle’s theorem for the functions: {tex}f(x)=\sin ^{4} x+\cos ^{4} x \text { in }\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex}
solution_text: We have {tex}f(x)=\sin ^{4} x+\cos ^{4} x i n\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex} .... (i) f(x) is continuous in {tex}\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex} [since, sin4x and cos4x are continuous functions and we know that, if g and h be continuous function, then (g + h) is a continuous function.] {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=4(\sin x)^{3} \cdot \cos x+4(\cos x)^{3} \cdot(-\sin x){/tex} {tex}=4 \sin ^{3} x \cdot \cos x-4 \sin x \cdot \cos ^{3} x{/tex} {tex}=4 \sin x \cos x\left(\sin ^{2} x-\cos ^{2} x\right){/tex} which exists in {tex}\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex} .... (ii) Hence, f(x) is differentiable in {tex}\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex} . Also, f(0) = 0 + 1 = 1 and {tex}f^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=1+0=1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow f(0)=f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow f(0)=f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex} Conditions of Rolle's theorem are satisfied. Hence, there eixsts atleast one {tex}c \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex} such that f'(c) = 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} 4sinc cosc (sin 2 c - cos 2 c) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4sinc cosc(-cos 2c) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow-2 \sin 2 c \cdot \cos 2 c=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -sin 4c = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin 4c = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow 4 c=\pi{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow c=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex} Hence, Rolle's theorem has been verified.
solution_html:
We have {tex}f(x)=\sin ^{4} x+\cos ^{4} x i n\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex} .... (i)

  1. f(x) is continuous in {tex}\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex}
    [since, sin4x and cos4x are continuous functions and we know that, if g and h be continuous function, then (g + h) is a continuous function.]
  2. {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=4(\sin x)^{3} \cdot \cos x+4(\cos x)^{3} \cdot(-\sin x){/tex}
    {tex}=4 \sin ^{3} x \cdot \cos x-4 \sin x \cdot \cos ^{3} x{/tex}
    {tex}=4 \sin x \cos x\left(\sin ^{2} x-\cos ^{2} x\right){/tex} which exists in {tex}\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex} .... (ii)
    Hence, f(x) is differentiable in {tex}\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex}.
  3. Also, f(0) = 0 + 1 = 1 and {tex}f^{\prime}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=1+0=1{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow f(0)=f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow f(0)=f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex}
    Conditions of Rolle's theorem are satisfied.

Hence, there eixsts atleast one {tex}c \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex} such that f'(c) = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 4sinc cosc (sin2c - cos2c) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4sinc cosc(-cos 2c) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow-2 \sin 2 c \cdot \cos 2 c=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -sin 4c = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin 4c = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow 4 c=\pi{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow c=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
and {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right){/tex}
Hence, Rolle's theorem has been verified.

question_text: Verify Rolle’s theorem for the functions: {tex}f(x) = log (x^2 + 2) – log ~3{/tex} in [–1, 1].
question_html: Verify Rolle’s theorem for the functions: {tex}f(x) = log (x^2 + 2) – log ~3{/tex} in [–1, 1].
solution_text: We have, {tex}f(x) = log(x^2 + 2) - log~3{/tex} Logarithmic functions are continuous in their domain. Hence, {tex}f(x) = log(x^2 + 2) - log~3{/tex} is continuous in [-1, 1] {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{x^{2}+2} \cdot 2 x-0{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+2}{/tex} which exists in (-1, 1) Hence, f(x) is differentiable in (-1, 1) f(-1) = log[(-1) 2 + 2] - log 3 = log 3 - log 3 = 0 and f(1) = log(1 2 + 2) - log 3 = log 3 - log 3 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(1) = f(1) Conditions Rolle's theorem are satisfied. Hence, there exists a real number c such that f'(c) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 c}{c^{2}+2}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow c=0 \in(-1,1){/tex} Hence, Rolle's theorem has been verified.
solution_html:
We have, {tex}f(x) = log(x^2 + 2) - log~3{/tex}

  1. Logarithmic functions are continuous in their domain.
    Hence, {tex}f(x) = log(x^2 + 2) - log~3{/tex} is continuous in [-1, 1]
  2. {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{x^{2}+2} \cdot 2 x-0{/tex}
    {tex}=\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+2}{/tex} which exists in (-1, 1)
    Hence, f(x) is differentiable in (-1, 1)
  3. f(-1) = log[(-1)2 + 2] - log 3 = log 3 - log 3 = 0
    and f(1) = log(12 + 2) - log 3 = log 3 - log 3 = 0
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(1) = f(1)
    Conditions Rolle's theorem are satisfied.

Hence, there exists a real number c such that
f'(c) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 c}{c^{2}+2}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow c=0 \in(-1,1){/tex}
Hence, Rolle's theorem has been verified.
